reviews.review_title: I wish they didn't quit on this.
reviews.review_description: I had such high hopes for the future of this game, then I came back to VR after a 5 month break to clear my head and I find out they stopped working on this gem I used to play in my free time for a game that in my opinion, doesn't live up to breakouts fun, and unique entities and monsters, where now with the other game it's just retextured monkey models like every other monkey horror game, but this game, no matter its status, shut down, or just abandoned, will be my top, and favorite monkey horror game.

Every time i play there is a live event and it never ends. Take fog for example its really bright and blinds from a far sight and imagine that but with 30 minutes of play it kinda ruins the game because when i ask players who joined before me they say that the live event ended. That is when i realized there was a glitch with the live events. 1. They were too common. 2. Whenever someone joined after the live event the player would see the live event and it would last the whole playthrough. 3. If you went to another server a different live evevt or the same live event would play. How i know this? it had happened to me countless times when i was playing and other people too. Someone was asking me why was the live event taking so long, but there was no live event the live event ended minutes ago. Mostly this game is fun but the live events are my problems. Then the jumpscares most of the monsters don't even have jumpscares but i would miss the," Your dead" jumpscare. But my problem is the character is so scary just to get a combat based jumpscare which instantly ruins the vibe if your a new player, the scariest jumpscare in my opinion is mutant's jumpscare its not that combat based so its not like your fighting the monster. If you make a monster jumpscare combat based then the player feels like they have power against the monster and its not scary anymore but, chatting with people hand trying to look good with cosmetics and. The way to get money is tedious so get crystals and then go to the other side of the map to the incinerator and then you'll get bucks.

reviews.review_title: Truly one of the BEST vr horror games.
reviews.review_description:
I'm going to give a few points as to why I LOVED it.

1) Monsters are SMART! They don't always INSTANTLY follow you UNLESS you are in front or too close, and if you close a door on them, they WILL find a way to get to you.

2) VERY fun money system. Instead of the old, annoying, and boring system where you get a certain amount of money every day, here you have to GRIND. You sell stuff for money; different things are worth different amounts of money.

3) REASONABLE PRICES. Cosmetics have good prices; you don't have to grind for days, but there also are a few very expensive cosmetics, which I think is nice. ALSO, paid add-ons can also be bought with credits, which are obtained by converting 250 bucks (Not too hard to grind; you can get them in <10 mins) into 1 credit, and add-ons can be bought for 15 - 30 credits.

Loving this game a lot

Breakout VR is hands down one of the most exciting virtual reality escape room games I’ve played. The puzzles are clever and challenging without being frustrating, and the environments are super detailed and immersive—it really feels like you’re inside the room solving everything in real time. The teamwork aspect makes it even more fun, and it’s perfect for friends or family who want a mix of brain-teasing challenges and thrilling VR action. Highly recommend to anyone who loves puzzles, escape rooms, or just wants a unique VR experience.
